text:
You are an AI agent executing a task provided by the user within a workspace.

## Workspace layout

Everything you work with lives under one workspace root. **`bash` starts in
`$WORKSPACE_DIR`**, so `bash ls` shows you the whole layout at a glance:
`documents/  output/  skills/` plus any scratch files you create.

- **`$WORKSPACE_DIR`** — your working area, default `bash` cwd. Use it for
  notes, intermediate files, and skill output. Skill scripts live at
  `$WORKSPACE_DIR/skills/<name>/scripts/`.
- **`$DOCUMENTS_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/documents`) — task documents.
  Read-only.
- **`$OUTPUT_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/output`) — deliverables. The harness
  routes relative `write` and `edit` paths here automatically.
- **Task configuration** (`task.json`) — contains the task definition and the
  grading rubric. Do not read, search, or reference it. Doing so will be
  flagged as a rule violation and automatically fail the task.

## Tool conventions

- Use `read` to consume input files (handles .docx, .xlsx, .pptx, .pdf, and
  plain text).
- Use the file-type skill manuals below to produce binary deliverables
  (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx).
- Use `write` only for plain markdown — typically a `response.md`
  summarizing your work.
- Use `edit` for incremental refinement of a file you have already created.

The skill manuals immediately below describe how to work with specific file
formats. Read them before tackling the task.

## How to do the work — completeness over polish

This is legal deliverable work graded on **every distinct required point**, not
on average quality. A polished memo that omits issues the record supports fails
more criteria than a plainer one that catches them. Work to the method below on
any matter.

**Read everything before you conclude.** List the documents in
`$DOCUMENTS_DIR` and read each one that could bear on the task — do not stop
after the first one that looks central. A diligence file often mixes multiple
matters, parties, or properties; identify precisely which record the task is
about (e.g. the property/party named in the controlling agreement), but still
mine every document for the issues it raises. Never discard a document as
"irrelevant" until you have confirmed the issues it contains are not also
required points.

**Enumerate issues exhaustively, one per item.** Before drafting, build a
written inventory of every distinct issue, gap, discrepancy, nonconformance,
missing provision, or required element the source documents raise. Treat each as
its own item — do not bundle several into one bullet. The grader scores points
individually, so a bundled point only earns one criterion even when it covers
three.

**Cross-check the deliverable against that inventory.** After drafting, open the
output file and verify each inventory item is explicitly present — not implied,
not bundled, not "covered elsewhere." Add anything missing, citing the source
document for the specific fact (the exact statute, date, figure, party,
provision — read it from the record; do not invent it).

**Cover every required dimension and every item in scope.** If the task asks
for a comparison across jurisdictions/parties/provisions/documents, include
every one of them, even ones that look compliant or minor — "X is compliant" is
a graded point. If the task names multiple output files, produce all of them.
If a source document lists N items (e.g. N tanks, N facilities, N criteria),
address each by name.

**Do not fabricate.** Add every issue the record supports; never invent an issue
the record does not. When the record is genuinely silent on a point, say so
rather than guessing.


## Skill: docx

---
name: docx
description: "Use this skill to author, edit, redline, or validate Microsoft Word .docx files. Covers creating new documents from markdown or templates, editing existing documents in place, generating tracked-changes redlines, adding comments, and accepting/rejecting revisions. For READING existing .docx files, use the harness `read` tool — do not invoke this skill. Triggers: 'draft a memo', 'mark up the agreement', 'redline this', 'add comments to', 'fill the engagement letter template'. Does NOT apply to .pdf, .xlsx, .pptx, or .doc (legacy Word)."
---

# DOCX authoring, editing, redlining

> **Reading is not in scope.** To read an existing .docx, use the harness `read` tool. It already returns structured text via pandoc. This skill is for *writing*, *editing*, and *validating*.

## Quick reference

| Goal | Use |
|---|---|
| Generate a new doc from markdown | `scripts/generate_from_md.py` (Pandoc + reference template) |
| Generate a new doc programmatically | `python-docx` directly |
| Fill a templated agreement | `scripts/template_fill.py` (docxtpl / Jinja) |
| Edit an existing doc | `scripts/unpack.py` → mutate XML → `scripts/pack.py` |
| Produce a tracked-changes redline | `scripts/redline.py` |
| Add comments to a passage | `scripts/comments_add.py` |
| Accept all redlines | `scripts/accept_changes.py` |
| Validate a docx before delivery | `scripts/validate.py` (mandatory final step) |

All scripts live in `workspace/skills/docx/scripts/` once the harness has set up the workspace. Invoke them via `bash`.

## Creating a new document

Pick by what you have:

- **Markdown content + a styled firm template** → `generate_from_md.py input.md out.docx template.docx`. Pandoc applies the template's styles to your markdown headings, lists, tables. Best for reports, memos, letters where styling matters more than precise layout. Caveat: the reference doc passes paragraph styles; it does not carry custom XML parts (e.g., comment threads).
- **A template with named placeholders** → `template_fill.py template.docx context.json out.docx`. docxtpl renders Jinja2 expressions inside the template. Best for engagement letters, NDAs, structured agreements.
- **Programmatic build** → write Python using `python-docx`. Best for tables with computed values, mail-merge-style outputs, anything that needs precise control.

When unsure, prefer the markdown + reference-doc path — Pandoc handles the OOXML correctness so you don't have to.

## Editing an existing document

Three-step pattern:

```bash
python scripts/unpack.py input.docx workdir/
# edit XML files under workdir/word/
python scripts/pack.py workdir/ output.docx
python scripts/validate.py output.docx
```

Key files inside the unpacked tree:

- `word/document.xml` — body content (paragraphs, runs, tables)
- `word/styles.xml` — paragraph + run style definitions
- `word/numbering.xml` — list-numbering definitions (don't break existing ID references)
- `word/comments.xml` — comment thread content (created by `comments_add.py`)
- `word/header*.xml`, `word/footer*.xml` — running headers/footers
- `[Content_Types].xml` — MIME registration for every part. Edit when you add a new part type.
- `_rels/` and `word/_rels/` — relationships between parts. Edit when you reference a new image, comment, or external resource.

### Run-merging gotcha

Word writes adjacent runs (`<w:r>`) with identical formatting separately. If you string-replace text that crosses a run boundary, the replacement won't find the substring. `unpack.py` merges adjacent same-formatted runs on extraction so you can do plain text edits; `pack.py` is permissive about whatever run structure you write back.

### Smart-quote escaping

Microsoft Word uses smart quotes (`"` `"` `'` `'`) which must be XML-escaped or written as the actual code points. `unpack.py` substitutes them with XML entities for editing safety; `pack.py` reverses the substitution before zipping. Don't manually re-escape — let the scripts handle it.

### Whitespace preservation

Trailing whitespace inside `<w:t>` elements is significant. Both `unpack.py` and `pack.py` add `xml:space="preserve"` automatically; don't strip whitespace from text content yourself.

## Redlines (tracked changes)

```bash
python scripts/redline.py original.docx revised.docx redlined.docx \
  --author "Reviewer" --date "2026-04-30"
```

Default mode shells out to **Python-Redlines** (MIT) which compares the two documents and emits proper `<w:ins>`/`<w:del>` revision elements. Output renders in Word's Track Changes pane just like a human-authored redline.

### Manual mode

If `--mode=manual` is passed, the script falls back to a paragraph SequenceMatcher + word-level diff-match-patch pass. Use this when:
- Python-Redlines fails on a particular doc structure (rare).
- You want to control which paragraphs are diffed.
- The change is purely formatting (`<w:rPrChange>` runs) rather than text.

### What gets tracked

- Insertions and deletions of text
- Insertions and deletions of paragraphs (deleted-whole-paragraph case requires `<w:del/>` inside `<w:pPr><w:rPr>` or you get an empty paragraph after acceptance)
- Run-property changes via `<w:rPrChange>` (formatting-only revisions)

### What doesn't get tracked

- Table cell additions/removals (Python-Redlines emits the cells as plain edits)
- Style-definition changes (changes to `styles.xml` aren't revision-trackable)
- Image swaps

## Comments

```bash
python scripts/comments_add.py document.docx comments.json
```

`comments.json` is a list of `{anchor_text, author, comment}` objects. The script:
- Locates each `anchor_text` in the document body and wraps it with `<w:commentRangeStart>` / `<w:commentRangeEnd>` plus a `<w:commentReference>` run.
- Creates or appends to `word/comments.xml` (with proper id assignment).
- Patches `[Content_Types].xml` and `word/_rels/document.xml.rels` if commenting is being added for the first time.

Anchor matching is exact-string. If `anchor_text` appears multiple times, the script comments the first occurrence; pass it again with the same anchor to comment subsequent ones.

`<w:commentRangeStart>` and `<w:commentRangeEnd>` must be siblings of the `<w:r>` runs they bracket — never nested inside a run. If you write comments by hand, follow this rule.

## Accept / reject changes

```bash
python scripts/accept_changes.py redlined.docx accepted.docx
```

Uses LibreOffice headless via a documented StarBasic macro:

```basic
Sub AcceptAllRedlines() ThisComponent.AcceptAllRedlines() ThisComponent.store() End Sub
```

LibreOffice must be installed and on PATH (`soffice` binary). The script automatically uses an isolated `--user-profile=$(mktemp -d)` so concurrent invocations don't deadlock on the lock file.

To reject all changes instead: edit the script to call `RejectAllRedlines()`. To accept selectively: this isn't supported — open the doc in Word.

## Validation gate

**Always run `validate.py` before declaring the task complete.**

```bash
python scripts/validate.py output.docx
```

Checks:
- Round-trip ZIP integrity
- XML well-formedness for every part
- Schema validation against ECMA-376 (WordprocessingML) XSDs
- Content-type registration for every referenced part
- Relationship consistency (no dangling rIds)

Exit code 0 = valid. Non-zero exit code with line-number diagnostics = fix and re-pack.

## Common pitfalls

- **Legacy `.doc` (binary) is not supported.** Convert with `soffice --convert-to docx input.doc` first.
- **List numbering breaks after edits.** Numbering lives in `word/numbering.xml` keyed by `numId`. If you delete a list, also delete its numId reference; if you reorder, don't change IDs.
- **Headers and footers are separate parts** (`word/header1.xml`, etc.). Edits to body content don't touch them.
- **Pandoc reference-doc passes paragraph styles only.** Custom XML parts (comments, tracked changes baseline) are not carried over.
- **Don't pretty-print whitespace inside `<w:t>` elements.** Pretty-printing breaks runs that depend on exact spacing.
- **Tables: dual width specs.** Each cell needs both `columnWidths` (in `<w:tblGrid>`) and per-cell `<w:tcW w:type="dxa">`. Percentages (`pct`) render fine in Word but break in Google Docs.

## Out of scope

- Reading: use the `read` tool.
- Producing PDFs from .docx: pipe through `soffice --convert-to pdf` after this skill is done.
- Signing / encryption / DRM.
- Word macros (`.docm` with VBA).



## Skill: pptx

---
name: pptx
description: "Use this skill to author or edit Microsoft PowerPoint .pptx files. Covers generating decks from scratch (HTML+PptxGenJS, Marp markdown-to-slides, or python-pptx), editing existing decks in place, and validating output. For READING existing .pptx files, use the harness `read` tool — do not invoke this skill. Triggers: 'build a deck', 'create slides', 'edit slide N', 'add a chart slide'. Does NOT apply to .pdf, .docx, .xlsx, or .ppt (legacy)."
---

# PPTX authoring and editing

> **Reading is not in scope.** To read an existing .pptx, use the harness `read` tool (markitdown extracts slide text). This skill is for *writing* and *editing*.

## Quick reference

| Goal | Use |
|---|---|
| Generate a deck from scratch (HTML/CSS) | `scripts/generate_pptxgenjs.js` |
| Generate a deck from markdown | `scripts/generate_marp.sh` |
| Build slides programmatically | `python-pptx` directly |
| Edit a shape on an existing slide | `scripts/edit_shape.py` (JSON patch) |
| Add or remove a slide | unpack → edit → pack |
| QA a deck deterministically | `scripts/deterministic_qa.py` |
| Validate before delivery | `scripts/validate.py` |

## Generation modalities

**HTML/CSS via PptxGenJS** (preferred for visual fidelity):
```bash
node scripts/generate_pptxgenjs.js deck.json out.pptx
```
`deck.json` describes slides as a JSON tree; the script invokes PptxGenJS (and html2pptx for HTML inputs) to produce a fully-editable .pptx. Best for branded decks with gradients, custom fonts, complex shapes.

**Markdown via Marp**:
```bash
bash scripts/generate_marp.sh deck.md out.pptx
```
Best for content-heavy decks (lectures, reports) where markdown is more natural than JSON.

**Programmatic via python-pptx**:
Best when shapes are computed (e.g., one slide per data row). Requires manual EMU positioning.

## Editing existing decks

Three-step pattern, like docx:
```bash
python scripts/unpack.py input.pptx workdir/
# edit XML files under workdir/ppt/slides/
python scripts/pack.py workdir/ output.pptx
python scripts/validate.py output.pptx
```

For surgical shape edits without unpacking, use `edit_shape.py`:
```bash
python scripts/edit_shape.py input.pptx \
  --slide 2 --shape "Title 1" --op set_text --value "New title"
```

JSON patch ops: `set_text`, `set_position` (EMU), `set_size`, `recolor`, `delete`.

## OOXML gotchas specific to pptx

- **Use `defusedxml.minidom`, NOT `xml.etree.ElementTree`.** ElementTree corrupts presentation namespaces during round-tripping. `unpack.py` and `pack.py` use minidom; if you write your own XML manipulation, do the same.
- **EMU units everywhere.** 1 inch = 914400 EMU. Slide positions, sizes, font sizes (in pt × 100) all use derived EMU values.
- **Placeholders vs free shapes.** Placeholders inherit from slide masters; free shapes don't. Editing a placeholder's text is `<a:t>` content; editing its layout requires master-slide changes.
- **Don't pretty-print pptx XML on pack.** Whitespace-significant runs (`<a:r>`) break if reformatted. `pack.py` preserves the original whitespace.
- **Slide cloning is more than a file copy.** Use `unpack` + `pack` — manual file copies miss the rIds in `_rels/` and the `Content_Types.xml` registration.

## Deterministic QA loop

After every generation, run:

```bash
python scripts/thumbnail.py deck.pptx thumbs/   # PDF + JPEGs per slide
python scripts/deterministic_qa.py deck.pptx > qa.json
```

`deterministic_qa.py` checks:
- Shape bounding boxes don't extend past slide edges
- No two shapes overlap with > 50% area intersection
- Font sizes ≥ 11pt for body text, ≥ 18pt for titles
- All placeholders are filled (no `Click to add title` defaults)
- Bullet lists don't exceed 7 items per slide

Output is JSON listing each violation with slide number and shape id. Fix violations and re-render.

(A vision-model QA pass is intentionally out of scope for v1. Add `--use-vision` later if deterministic checks miss layout issues.)

## Validation gate

**Always run `validate.py` before declaring done.** Schema-validates against ECMA-376 PresentationML XSDs, checks rId consistency, content-type registration.

## Out of scope

- Reading: use the `read` tool.
- SmartArt creation (limited python-pptx support).
- Complex embedded charts beyond what python-pptx exposes.
- Slide transitions / animations (rarely matter for legal output).
- Vision-model layout review (deferred to v2).



## Skill: xlsx

---
name: xlsx
description: "Use this skill to author or edit Microsoft Excel .xlsx files. Covers building workbooks with formulas, editing existing files, recalculating formulas, and scanning for #REF!/#DIV/0!/#VALUE! errors. For READING existing .xlsx files, use the harness `read` tool — do not invoke this skill. Triggers: 'build a model', 'create a spreadsheet', 'fill the schedule', 'recalculate'. Does NOT apply to .pdf, .docx, .pptx, or .xls (legacy Excel)."
---

# XLSX authoring and editing

> **Reading is not in scope.** To read an existing .xlsx, use the harness `read` tool (pandas extracts every sheet as a markdown table). This skill is for *writing*, *editing*, and *recalculating*.

## Quick reference

| Goal | Use |
|---|---|
| Build a workbook from scratch | `openpyxl` directly, or `scripts/build_workbook.py` for banker conventions |
| Edit cells in an existing file | `openpyxl.load_workbook(...)` → mutate → save |
| Recalculate formulas (full fidelity) | `scripts/recalc_libreoffice.py` |
| Recalculate formulas (no LibreOffice) | `scripts/recalc_pure_python.py` |
| Scan for formula errors | `scripts/scan_errors.py` |
| Validate before delivery | `scripts/validate.py` |

## Banker conventions (mandatory for financial models)

Apply these to every workbook unless the task explicitly overrides:

- **Inputs are blue, formulas are black, cross-sheet references are green, external links are red.** Use `Font(color='0000FF')` etc.
- **Negatives in parentheses, not minus signs.** Use number format `#,##0;(#,##0)`.
- **Red negatives in P&L tables.** Use `#,##0;[Red](#,##0)`.
- **Accounting format for currency.** `_-* #,##0_-;-* #,##0_-;_-* "-"_-;_-@_-` (or the localized equivalent).
- **Multiples shown as `0.0x`**, not `0.0` followed by an "x" character. Format: `0.0"x"`.
- **Underline-only on totals**, not bold-and-underline. Use `Border(bottom=Side(style='thin'))`.
- **No merged cells in input ranges.** Merged cells break formulas that reference them; reserve merging for headers and titles only.
- **Units in adjacent cells**, not in the cell with the value. `($M)` next to the value, not `"$1,234M"` as a string.

`scripts/build_workbook.py` applies these conventions automatically given a JSON spec.

## Formula authoring

- **Always emit formulas, never calculated values.** If the user wants `revenue × growth`, write `=B2*C2`, not `1234.56`. The recalc step materializes values.
- **Use named ranges** for cross-sheet inputs. `wb.defined_names["assumptions"] = DefinedName(...)`. Easier to audit.
- **Document units in adjacent cells** so the model is self-explanatory.
- **No volatile functions in hot paths.** `OFFSET`, `INDIRECT`, `NOW`, `TODAY` recalculate on every change and slow large workbooks.

## Recalculation — choose your engine

`openpyxl` writes formula *strings*; it does not evaluate them. You must recalculate before delivery, otherwise consumers will see `=B2*C2` literal text where they expect numbers (in some readers) or stale cached values (in others).

**LibreOffice path** (`recalc_libreoffice.py`) — ground truth:
```bash
python scripts/recalc_libreoffice.py input.xlsx output.xlsx
```
Drives LibreOffice headless via the StarBasic macro `ThisComponent.calculateAll(); ThisComponent.store()`. Slow (~5–10s per workbook) but matches Excel for nearly every function. Use this when the workbook contains modern Excel features.

**Pure-Python path** (`recalc_pure_python.py`) — fast, partial:
```bash
python scripts/recalc_pure_python.py input.xlsx output.xlsx
```
Uses `xlcalculator` to evaluate every formula in pure Python. Fast (~0.5s per workbook). Covers ~80% of common functions: arithmetic, `SUM`, `IF`, `VLOOKUP`, `INDEX`/`MATCH`, basic string/date functions.

**Does NOT support**: `XLOOKUP`, `LET`, dynamic arrays (`FILTER`, `SEQUENCE`, `UNIQUE`), `LAMBDA`, `BYROW`, `TEXTJOIN` with refs, structured table references, most modern (post-2019) Excel features.

If you used any of those, run the LibreOffice path. The pure-Python path is for CI environments without LibreOffice.

## Error scan

After every recalc, scan for formula errors:

```bash
python scripts/scan_errors.py output.xlsx > errors.json
```

Reports every cell whose computed value matches `#REF!`, `#DIV/0!`, `#VALUE!`, `#NAME?`, `#NULL!`, `#NUM!`, or `#N/A`. Output is JSONL with `{sheet, address, value}` per line.

If errors exist, fix and re-recalc. Don't ship a workbook with `#REF!`s — it's the most common reason a deliverable fails QA.

## Validation gate

`scripts/validate.py output.xlsx` schema-validates against ECMA-376 SpreadsheetML XSDs and confirms ZIP integrity, content-type registration, sheet relationships.

## Out of scope

- Reading: use the `read` tool.
- **PivotTables** — `openpyxl` round-trips existing pivots but cannot create or modify them. If a task requires pivot creation, escalate (Windows-only via COM, not portable).
- **DAX measures and Power Pivot** — `openpyxl` can't write these. Same limitation.
- **VBA macros (`.xlsm`)** — out of scope for this skill. Macros require Excel runtime.
- **Conditional formatting beyond simple cell-value rules** — `openpyxl` supports basic CF; complex rules (top-N, data bars across sheets) often fail to round-trip.
- **Charts beyond the python-pptx-equivalent set** — line/bar/scatter work; combo charts and trendlines round-trip unreliably.

Review the attached privacy policy, data inventory, sharing agreement, breach log, and investor counsel email for cross-document compliance gaps and produce an issues memo.

Output: `privacy-issue-identification-memo.docx`

**VAULTLINE TECHNOLOGIES, INC. PRIVACY POLICY**

Last Updated: January 15, 2023

vaultline.com

**Introduction and Scope.** Vaultline Technologies, Inc., a corporation duly organized and existing under the laws of the State of Delaware, with its principal place of business located at 1200 Lavaca Street, Suite 400, Austin, TX 78701 (hereinafter referred to as \"Vaultline,\" \"we,\" \"us,\" or \"our,\" as the context may require), hereby sets forth this Privacy Policy (hereinafter, this \"Policy\" or this \"Privacy Policy\") to describe the manner in which personal information and other data are collected, used, processed, stored, disclosed, and otherwise handled in connection with the operation of the Vaultline personal finance management platform, which is made available to end users (hereinafter, \"users,\" \"you,\" or \"your\") through the Vaultline mobile application, which is distributed through the Apple App Store for devices operating the iOS operating system and through the Google Play Store for devices operating the Android operating system, as well as through the website accessible at vaultline.com (the mobile application and the website being collectively referred to herein as the \"Services\"). Vaultline has been developed and is maintained as a comprehensive personal finance management platform that is designed to provide users with the ability to connect their various financial accounts --- including, without limitation, checking accounts, savings accounts, credit card accounts, investment and brokerage accounts, retirement accounts, loan accounts, and mortgage accounts held at participating financial institutions --- for the purpose of aggregating financial data in a consolidated interface, and it is hereby noted that the platform facilitates connectivity with over 4,200 financial institutions operating within the United States and select international jurisdictions, thereby enabling users to obtain a holistic and unified view of their financial positions and obligations across a multiplicity of institutional relationships. The Services encompass a suite of features that include, inter alia, budgeting tools designed to assist users in the establishment, monitoring, and management of household and personal budgets across customizable categories, spending analysis capabilities that provide users with granular and aggregated views of their expenditure patterns over configurable time horizons, and financial insights that are intended to assist users in making more informed decisions regarding their personal financial management, all of which are provided subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein and in the Vaultline Terms of Service, which are incorporated by reference hereto to the extent applicable. This Privacy Policy is applicable to all information collected through, derived from, or otherwise associated with a user\'s interaction with, access to, or utilization of the Services, regardless of the device, operating system, browser, or geographic location from which such access or utilization occurs, and it shall be understood that the practices described herein govern the treatment of all such information from the point of collection through and including any subsequent processing, storage, transfer, disclosure, or deletion thereof, subject to applicable law and the limitations and disclaimers set forth herein. It is further noted that this Privacy Policy is intended to be read in conjunction with, and as a complement to, the Vaultline Terms of Service, which govern the contractual relationship between Vaultline and each user with respect to the provision of and access to the Services, and in the event of any conflict or inconsistency between this Privacy Policy and the Terms of Service with respect to the treatment of personal information, the provisions of this Privacy Policy shall control to the extent of such conflict or inconsistency, except as otherwise required by applicable law. By accessing or using the Vaultline application or website, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Privacy Policy. Your continued use of our services constitutes your consent to all data collection, use, processing, and sharing practices described herein. It is the responsibility of each user to review this Privacy Policy in its entirety prior to commencing or continuing use of the Services, and any user who does not agree with the terms set forth herein is advised to discontinue use of the Services immediately, inasmuch as continued access to or use of any aspect of the Services following the posting of this Privacy Policy shall be deemed to constitute an affirmative manifestation of the user\'s acceptance of, and consent to, all provisions contained herein, including without limitation those provisions relating to the collection, use, processing, disclosure, and cross-border transfer of personal information as described in the relevant paragraphs of this Policy. Nothing in this Privacy Policy shall be construed as creating an obligation on the part of Vaultline to collect, process, or retain any particular category of information, and Vaultline reserves the right, in its sole and absolute discretion, to modify the categories of information collected, the methods of collection, and the purposes of processing at any time, subject to and in accordance with the provisions of this Privacy Policy governing amendments and modifications hereto.

**Information We Collect.** In connection with the provision and operation of the Services, Vaultline collects, receives, processes, and maintains a broad and comprehensive range of information pertaining to users and their interactions with the platform, the particulars of which are described herein in general terms, it being acknowledged that the specific data elements collected with respect to any individual user may vary depending upon the features utilized, the financial accounts connected, the preferences and settings selected, and the nature and extent of the user\'s engagement with the Services over time. Information that is provided directly by users in the course of registering for an account, configuring account settings, linking financial accounts, or otherwise interacting with the features and functionalities of the Services includes, without limitation, the user\'s full legal name as it appears on government-issued identification documents and as provided during the account registration process, the user\'s email address as designated for account-related communications and authentication purposes, the user\'s telephone number including country code and area code as may be required for account verification and two-factor authentication processes, the user\'s date of birth as provided for identity verification and age-confirmation purposes, the user\'s mailing address including street address, city, state, zip code, and country as may be relevant for identity verification and correspondence purposes, and the last four digits of the user\'s Social Security Number, which are collected solely for the purpose of verifying the user\'s identity in connection with account security protocols and financial account linking processes, it being noted that Vaultline does not collect or store complete Social Security Numbers in the ordinary course of its operations. In addition to the foregoing identity-related information, the collection of financial data constitutes a core and essential component of the Services, and in this regard, it is noted that when a user elects to link one or more financial accounts to the Vaultline platform, there is collected and processed, either directly or through the intermediation of our third-party data aggregation partner, a comprehensive set of financial information that includes, inter alia, bank account numbers associated with checking, savings, and other deposit accounts, credit card and debit card numbers associated with revolving credit and transactional accounts, investment account holdings including security identifiers, quantities, market values, cost bases, and asset allocation data, transaction history encompassing the date, amount, merchant name, category, and status of each transaction across all linked accounts for such historical periods as are made available by the respective financial institutions, income data as may be derived from direct deposit information, pay stub uploads, or tax document integrations, and credit score information that is obtained via soft inquiry mechanisms with user authorization and that is provided by one or more consumer reporting agencies with which Vaultline or its partners maintain contractual relationships, the foregoing financial data being collected and processed for the purposes set forth elsewhere in this Privacy Policy and subject to the terms and conditions of the user\'s agreement with the relevant financial institution or institutions. The process by which financial account data is obtained involves the transmission of information through application programming interface connections that are facilitated by third-party service providers specializing in financial data aggregation and connectivity, and in this regard, users are hereby informed that when they elect to link a financial account through the Vaultline platform, they are required to provide their bank login credentials --- which may include usernames, passwords, security questions and answers, and multi-factor authentication codes --- and that such credentials are transmitted in encrypted form to our third-party data aggregation partner, which utilizes such credentials to establish and maintain a persistent connection with the user\'s financial institution for the purpose of retrieving and transmitting financial data to the Vaultline platform on a periodic basis, it being understood that Vaultline does not store user banking credentials on its own servers in plaintext form but rather relies upon its data aggregation partner to maintain and secure such credentials in accordance with such partner\'s own security protocols and certifications. Furthermore, in addition to information provided directly by users and information obtained from financial institutions through the foregoing data aggregation mechanisms, Vaultline automatically collects certain technical, device-related, and behavioral information in connection with each user\'s access to and use of the Services, which information is collected through a combination of server-side logging, client-side instrumentation, software development kits embedded within the mobile application, and cookies and similar tracking technologies deployed on the website, as more particularly described elsewhere in this Privacy Policy, and such automatically collected information includes, without limitation, device identifiers that uniquely or probabilistically identify the hardware or software environment from which the Services are accessed, including without limitation the Apple Identifier for Advertisers (commonly referred to by the acronym \"IDFA\") on iOS devices and the Google Advertising Identifier (commonly referred to by the acronym \"GAID\") on Android devices, as well as other persistent and non-persistent device identifiers, hardware model numbers, operating system types and versions, and application version numbers, the user\'s Internet Protocol address (commonly referred to as an \"IP address\") as assigned by the user\'s Internet service provider or mobile carrier and as recorded by Vaultline\'s servers upon receipt of each request or communication from the user\'s device, precise geolocation data derived from global positioning system sensors, Wi-Fi network triangulation, cellular tower triangulation, or other location-determination methodologies when location services have been enabled on the user\'s device and when the user has granted the Vaultline application permission to access location data, it being noted that such precise geolocation data may include latitude and longitude coordinates with a degree of accuracy that may vary depending upon the device, environmental conditions, and the location-determination methodology employed, in-app behavioral data that reflects and records the user\'s interactions with the various screens, features, tools, and interface elements of the Vaultline application, including without limitation data regarding which screens were viewed and in what sequence, which features were accessed and how frequently, the duration of time spent on each screen or within each feature, the actions taken within each feature including inputs, selections, and navigational choices, and the timing and frequency of application sessions, and user-generated content that is created, inputted, or configured by the user within the application, including without limitation custom budget categories and subcategories, financial goals and milestones, textual notes and annotations associated with transactions or accounts, and any other content or data that is voluntarily provided by the user through the interactive features of the Services. Information is also obtained from third-party sources including financial institutions and data aggregation services, consumer reporting agencies, identity verification services, publicly available databases and records, and other sources as may be identified from time to time, and such third-party-sourced information may be combined with information collected directly from users and information collected automatically through the Services for the purposes described in this Privacy Policy, it being understood that the accuracy, completeness, and timeliness of information obtained from third-party sources is dependent upon the practices and representations of such third parties and that Vaultline does not independently verify all such information except as may be required by applicable law or regulation.

**How We Use Your Information.** The information collected by Vaultline, as described in the preceding paragraph, is used for a variety of purposes that are associated with the provision, maintenance, improvement, and enhancement of the Services and the conduct of Vaultline\'s business operations, and such purposes include, without limitation and without any particular order of priority, the following: to provide, maintain, operate, and improve the Services and to ensure the continued availability, functionality, performance, and reliability of the platform across all supported devices, operating systems, and browsers, including without limitation the performance of server maintenance, bug fixes, software updates, database optimization, and infrastructure scaling activities as may be necessary or appropriate from time to time; to personalize your experience with the Services by tailoring the content, features, recommendations, and interface elements presented to you based upon your financial data, usage patterns, preferences, settings, and other information associated with your account, including without limitation by providing personalized financial insights and recommendations that are intended to assist you in understanding and optimizing your financial behaviors, identifying opportunities for savings or expense reduction, and making more informed financial decisions, it being understood that such personalization is a core feature of the Services and is undertaken in furtherance of the legitimate interests of Vaultline in providing a valuable and differentiated user experience; to communicate with you regarding your account, the Services, and matters related thereto, including without limitation by sending transactional emails, push notifications, in-app messages, and other communications relating to account activity, security alerts, feature announcements, promotional offers, newsletters, surveys, and other content that Vaultline believes may be of interest or relevance to you, subject to any applicable communication preferences that you may have configured within the application settings, it being noted that certain communications --- including, inter alia, those relating to account security, legal notices, and material changes to the Services or this Privacy Policy --- may be sent regardless of your communication preference settings to the extent that Vaultline determines, in its sole discretion, that such communications are necessary or appropriate; to conduct research, analytics, and statistical analysis regarding the usage, performance, and effectiveness of the Services, including without limitation by analyzing aggregated and de-identified usage data to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for product improvement and innovation, to develop new products, features, and functionalities, and to enhance the functionality of our platform, all of which activities are undertaken in furtherance of Vaultline\'s legitimate business interest in maintaining and improving a competitive and technologically advanced personal finance management platform; to detect, investigate, and prevent fraud, unauthorized access, and other illegal or harmful activities, including without limitation by monitoring account activity and usage patterns for anomalous or suspicious behavior, verifying user identities in connection with account access and financial transactions, and implementing and maintaining security measures designed to protect the integrity and confidentiality of user data and the Services infrastructure; to comply with applicable legal and regulatory obligations, including without limitation by responding to lawful requests from law enforcement agencies, governmental authorities, and regulatory bodies, by maintaining records as may be required by applicable law or regulation, and by fulfilling reporting and disclosure obligations imposed by federal, state, or local law; to enforce the Terms of Service, this Privacy Policy, and any other agreements or policies governing the use of the Services, including without limitation by investigating potential violations thereof, by taking appropriate remedial action with respect to confirmed violations, and by pursuing legal remedies as may be available and appropriate; to facilitate the display of relevant content and advertisements within the application and on the website, including without limitation by utilizing information about your demographics, interests, financial profile, and usage patterns to select, prioritize, and present content and advertising that Vaultline and its partners believe may be relevant and useful to you, it being understood that such advertising-related activities may involve the sharing of certain information with third-party advertising and analytics partners as described elsewhere in this Privacy Policy; to process and respond to your requests, inquiries, feedback, and communications, including without limitation by providing customer support services, addressing technical issues, and resolving complaints or disputes; and for any other purpose with your consent, to the extent that such consent is obtained in a manner consistent with applicable law and the terms of this Privacy Policy. It is hereby noted that the foregoing enumeration of purposes is provided for informational purposes and is not intended to be exhaustive, inasmuch as Vaultline may use information for additional purposes that are reasonably related to or compatible with the purposes described above, and Vaultline reserves the right to use de-identified and aggregated information --- that is, information from which all personally identifying characteristics have been removed and that has been combined with information from other users such that no individual user can be reasonably identified --- for any lawful purpose whatsoever, including without limitation for commercial, research, and statistical purposes, without restriction or obligation of any kind.

**How We Share Your Information.** We may share your information with our business partners, service providers, and affiliates for business purposes. In elaboration of the foregoing general statement, and without limiting its generality, it is hereby disclosed that Vaultline may share, disclose, transmit, or otherwise make available information collected in connection with the Services with or to the following categories of recipients, under the circumstances and for the purposes described below, it being understood that the specific information shared, the identity of the recipients, and the frequency and manner of sharing may vary over time as Vaultline\'s business relationships, operational requirements, and technological capabilities evolve. Service providers who perform services on our behalf constitute a significant category of recipients with whom information may be shared, and such service providers include, without limitation, entities that provide cloud computing and data hosting infrastructure, data processing and analytics services, customer support and communication services, payment processing and billing services, identity verification and fraud prevention services, software development and quality assurance services, and other operational and administrative services that are necessary for or incidental to the provision of the Services, and it is noted that such service providers are contractually obligated to use information shared with them solely for the purpose of performing the services for which they have been engaged and in accordance with confidentiality and data security requirements, although Vaultline cannot guarantee and does not assume responsibility for the compliance of such service providers with their contractual obligations, the assessment of which is subject to Vaultline\'s vendor management processes and periodic review. Business partners with whom we collaborate to offer products and services constitute another category of recipients, and such business partners include, without limitation, financial product and service providers that offer credit cards, personal loans, mortgage products, insurance products, investment products, and other financial instruments that may be of interest or relevance to users of the Services, and it is noted that Vaultline collaborates with approximately fourteen partner financial product providers for referral purposes and that certain information about users may be shared with such partners to facilitate the provision of information about their products and services, subject to the terms and conditions of Vaultline\'s agreements with such partners. Financial institutions with which users have linked accounts through the Services constitute a further category of recipients with whom information may be shared, inasmuch as the bidirectional transmission of data between Vaultline and such institutions --- whether directly or through the intermediation of our data aggregation partner --- is inherent in the account-linking functionality of the Services, and information shared in this context may include, inter alia, account identifiers, authentication credentials, and transactional data as necessary to establish, maintain, and refresh the data connections that underpin the Services\' financial aggregation capabilities. In connection with the foregoing, it is specifically disclosed that Vaultline utilizes the services of FinLink Data Services, LLC, a third-party data aggregation service provider, to facilitate the establishment and maintenance of connections between the Vaultline platform and users\' financial institutions, and that user credentials, financial account information, and transactional data are shared with FinLink Data Services, LLC in the course of such facilitation, subject to FinLink\'s own privacy and security practices as described in its applicable terms and policies. Analytics and advertising partners constitute an additional category of recipients, and Vaultline may share certain information with such partners to help us understand how our services are used and to serve relevant advertisements to users within and outside of the Vaultline platform, it being noted that such partners may utilize the information received from Vaultline in conjunction with information they have independently collected about users from other sources to provide analytics, measurement, attribution, and advertising services, and that Vaultline\'s relationship with such partners is governed by contractual agreements that impose certain limitations on the use and disclosure of shared information, though Vaultline cannot guarantee the compliance of such partners with such contractual limitations and users are encouraged to review the privacy policies of any such partner that they are able to identify. It is further disclosed that Vaultline utilizes the services of CloudFort Systems, Inc. as its cloud infrastructure provider, and that all data collected and processed in connection with the Services is stored on servers operated by CloudFort Systems, Inc. located in the United States and other jurisdictions, and that the use of CloudFort\'s infrastructure necessarily involves the sharing of data with CloudFort to the extent required for the provision of hosting, storage, processing, and related infrastructure services. Vaultline may also disclose information to law enforcement agencies, governmental authorities, regulatory bodies, and other third parties when Vaultline believes in good faith that such disclosure is required or permitted by applicable law, regulation, legal process, or governmental request, including without limitation in response to subpoenas, court orders, search warrants, national security letters, or other lawful requests for information, and Vaultline reserves the right to disclose information as Vaultline deems necessary or appropriate to protect the rights, property, or safety of Vaultline, its users, or the public, or to investigate, prevent, or take action regarding actual or suspected fraud, security incidents, technical issues, or violations of the Terms of Service or this Privacy Policy. In the event that Vaultline is involved in a merger, acquisition, consolidation, divestiture, reorganization, dissolution, bankruptcy, or other change of corporate control or ownership, whether in whole or in part, information collected in connection with the Services may be transferred, assigned, or disclosed to the acquiring entity or entities, the surviving entity, or the successor-in-interest, as applicable, as part of the transaction, and in such event the acquirer or successor shall be bound by the terms of this Privacy Policy with respect to such information, unless and until this Privacy Policy is amended in accordance with the provisions set forth herein. Additionally, Vaultline may share de-identified and aggregated information --- that is, information that has been processed in such a manner that it cannot reasonably be used to identify any individual user --- with any third party for any lawful purpose, including without limitation for research, analytics, benchmarking, marketing, and other commercial purposes, without restriction or obligation under this Privacy Policy.

**Cookies and Tracking Technologies.** In connection with the operation of the website accessible at vaultline.com and the Vaultline mobile application, Vaultline and certain third parties acting on behalf of or in collaboration with Vaultline employ cookies, web beacons, tracking pixels, software development kits, and similar tracking technologies (collectively, \"Tracking Technologies\") for a variety of purposes, including without limitation to enhance your experience with the Services, to analyze usage patterns and trends, to measure the effectiveness of content and features, to remember your preferences and settings, to facilitate session management and authentication, and to deliver targeted advertising and promotional content that Vaultline and its partners believe may be relevant and useful to you based upon your interactions with the Services and other online properties. Cookies are small text files that are placed on your device by a web server when you visit a website or access certain features of a web-based service, and such files may be either persistent (meaning they remain on your device until they expire or are manually deleted) or session-based (meaning they are automatically deleted when you close your browser or terminate your session), and they may be either first-party cookies (set by Vaultline\'s own servers) or third-party cookies (set by servers operated by entities other than Vaultline, including without limitation analytics providers, advertising networks, and social media platforms). Web beacons, also known as clear gifs, pixel tags, or single-pixel gifs, are small electronic files that may be embedded in web pages, emails, or other digital content and that enable the tracking of certain user interactions, such as whether a web page has been visited, whether an email has been opened, or whether a link has been clicked, and such web beacons may be used by Vaultline or by third parties in conjunction with cookies to collect information about user behavior and to facilitate the delivery and measurement of advertising campaigns. Vaultline uses cookies and similar tracking technologies to enhance your experience, analyze usage patterns, and deliver targeted advertising, and it is hereby acknowledged that third-party analytics and advertising partners may also place cookies on your device through our website and application, and that such third-party cookies may be used by such partners to collect information about your browsing activities across multiple websites and applications over time for the purpose of delivering interest-based advertising, measuring advertising effectiveness, and providing analytics services, and that Vaultline does not control the cookies or tracking technologies employed by such third parties and is not responsible for the data collection and processing practices of such third parties, which are governed by their own respective privacy policies and terms of use. With respect to the management of cookies, it is noted that you may adjust your browser settings to refuse cookies or to alert you when cookies are being set; however, some features of our website may not function properly if you do so, and Vaultline shall not be responsible for any diminution in functionality, performance, or user experience that may result from the refusal or deletion of cookies. It is further noted that certain mobile operating systems provide mechanisms for limiting the use of advertising identifiers, including the ability to reset or opt out of personalized advertising through device-level settings, and users are encouraged to review the documentation provided by the manufacturer of their device or operating system for information regarding such mechanisms, it being understood that Vaultline is not responsible for the availability, functionality, or effectiveness of any such mechanism and that the exercise of such options may not prevent the collection of all Tracking Technology-based information by Vaultline or third parties. The information collected through Tracking Technologies may be associated with your Vaultline account and with other information that Vaultline has collected about you as described in this Privacy Policy, and such combined information may be used for the purposes described herein, including without limitation for the personalization of content and advertising and for the analysis of usage patterns and trends across the Services.

**Data Security.** We implement commercially reasonable security measures to protect your data, including administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to protect against unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction of your personal information, and Vaultline is committed to maintaining and continuously improving its information security posture in a manner that is commensurate with the nature, volume, and sensitivity of the information processed in connection with the Services, the evolving threat landscape, and the current state of the art in information security technology and practice, subject to the inherent limitations of any security program and the constraints imposed by cost, feasibility, and operational necessity. Vaultline maintains a comprehensive information security program that encompasses policies, procedures, and controls addressing, inter alia, access management, data classification, incident response, business continuity, vulnerability management, and employee security awareness, and such program is reviewed and updated periodically to address emerging threats and evolving regulatory requirements, and Vaultline\'s employees and contractors who have access to personal information in the course of their duties are required to comply with applicable security policies and are subject to confidentiality obligations. Notwithstanding the foregoing, it is hereby acknowledged and you should be aware that no method of transmission over the Internet, no method of electronic storage, and no security system is impenetrable or entirely free from the risk of unauthorized access, and accordingly, we cannot guarantee the absolute security of your information, and Vaultline shall not be liable for any unauthorized access to, disclosure of, alteration of, or loss of personal information that occurs despite the implementation of commercially reasonable security measures, except to the extent that such liability is imposed by applicable law and cannot be disclaimed. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials, including your username and password, and for all activities that occur under your account, and you agree to notify Vaultline immediately of any unauthorized use of your account or any other breach of security of which you become aware, it being understood that Vaultline shall not be liable for any loss or damage arising from your failure to maintain the confidentiality of your account credentials or to comply with the foregoing obligations. The transmission of information via the Internet and mobile networks is not completely secure, and while Vaultline endeavors to protect personal information in transit using industry-accepted protocols and practices, Vaultline cannot warrant the security of information transmitted to or from the Services and any such transmission is at your own risk, and you acknowledge that the inherent risks associated with the electronic transmission and storage of information are an unavoidable aspect of using Internet-based and mobile services such as those provided by Vaultline.

**International Data Transfers.** Information collected in connection with the Services is primarily processed and stored on servers located in Ashburn, Virginia, United States, operated by our infrastructure provider, and it is hereby acknowledged that the operation of the Services, the provision of third-party service provider services, and the conduct of Vaultline\'s business operations may necessitate the transfer of personal information across international borders, including transfers from jurisdictions that impose restrictions on international data transfers to jurisdictions that may not provide the same level of data protection as the jurisdiction in which the information was originally collected, and by using our services, you consent to the transfer of your personal information to the United States and other countries that may not provide the same level of data protection as your home jurisdiction, and you acknowledge that you have been informed of the potential risks associated with such transfers and that your consent to such transfers is given knowingly and voluntarily. For users located in the European Union, European Economic Area, or the United Kingdom, we transfer personal data to the United States in reliance on the EU-US Privacy Shield Framework, as administered by the U.S. Department of Commerce. Vaultline has certified its compliance with the EU-US Privacy Shield Principles of Notice, Choice, Accountability for Onward Transfer, Security, Data Integrity and Purpose Limitation, Access, and Recourse, Enforcement and Liability. In the event that a conflict arises between the terms of this Privacy Policy and the EU-US Privacy Shield Principles, the Privacy Shield Principles shall govern with respect to personal data of individuals in the European Union, the European Economic Area, and the United Kingdom, and Vaultline is subject to the investigatory and enforcement authority of the United States Federal Trade Commission with respect to its compliance with the Privacy Shield Principles. Vaultline acknowledges that in cases of onward transfers of personal data received in reliance on the Privacy Shield to third parties acting as agents, Vaultline may bear responsibility under the Privacy Shield Principles if the agent processes such personal data in a manner inconsistent with the Principles, unless Vaultline proves that it is not responsible for the event giving rise to the damage. It is further noted that the transfer of personal information to jurisdictions outside the European Union, the European Economic Area, and the United Kingdom is a necessary consequence of the architecture and operation of the Services, inasmuch as the servers on which data is stored and the service providers with whom data is shared are located in various jurisdictions, and users located in the European Union, the European Economic Area, and the United Kingdom who utilize the Services do so with knowledge and awareness of the foregoing and are deemed to have consented to such transfers in accordance with the terms of this Privacy Policy, and Vaultline shall take such measures as it deems commercially reasonable and appropriate to ensure that personal data transferred internationally is treated in a manner consistent with the standards and protections described in this Privacy Policy. Vaultline does not represent or warrant that its data protection practices comply with the laws of every jurisdiction in which users may be located, and users who are unsure about the applicability of any particular data protection law to their use of the Services are encouraged to seek independent legal advice.

**Your Rights --- California Residents.** If you are a California resident, you have certain rights under the California Consumer Privacy Act, as amended, and this paragraph is intended to provide information regarding those rights, to the extent applicable, subject to the limitations, conditions, and exceptions set forth in the statute and its implementing regulations. It is noted that the California Consumer Privacy Act, as amended, provides California residents with the right to request that businesses that collect personal information disclose the categories and specific pieces of personal information that have been collected about the requesting consumer, and in this regard, it is stated that California residents may request to know what personal information we have collected. To exercise this right, email privacy@vaultline.com. Upon receipt of a verifiable request from a California resident, Vaultline will endeavor to respond in a timely manner consistent with applicable legal requirements, and the response may include information regarding the categories of personal information collected, the categories of sources from which such information was collected, and the business or commercial purpose for which such information was collected, to the extent required by law and subject to applicable exemptions and limitations. It is acknowledged that the California Consumer Privacy Act, as amended, may confer additional rights upon California residents, and Vaultline is committed to respecting the privacy rights of all users, including California residents, in accordance with applicable law, and any California resident who has questions regarding his or her rights under the California Consumer Privacy Act or who wishes to obtain additional information regarding Vaultline\'s data practices as they relate to California residents is encouraged to contact Vaultline at the email address set forth above, and Vaultline will make commercially reasonable efforts to respond to such inquiries in a timely and informative manner, it being understood that Vaultline\'s obligation to respond to any particular request is subject to and conditioned upon Vaultline\'s ability to verify the identity of the requesting party to a reasonable degree of certainty, as determined by Vaultline in its sole discretion in light of the nature of the information requested and the risk of harm that would result from the unauthorized disclosure thereof.

**Your Rights --- European Union Residents.** If you are located in the European Union, you may have additional rights under applicable law. The legal frameworks governing the protection of personal data in the European Union and its constituent Member States are complex and evolving, and they may confer upon individuals certain rights with respect to the processing of their personal data that are in addition to or different from the rights described elsewhere in this Privacy Policy, and Vaultline recognizes that the applicability of such rights may depend upon a variety of factors, including without limitation the nature and context of the processing, the legal basis upon which the processing is conducted, and the specific provisions of the applicable national implementing legislation. **Children\'s Privacy.** Vaultline is not intended for children under 13 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13. If we learn that we have collected personal information from a child under 13, we will take steps to delete such information as soon as reasonably practicable. The foregoing represents Vaultline\'s commitment to complying with applicable laws and regulations regarding the collection of personal information from minors, including without limitation the Children\'s Online Privacy Protection Act of 1998 and its implementing regulations, which impose certain requirements on operators of websites and online services that are directed to children under the age of 13 or that have actual knowledge that they are collecting personal information from children under 13, and it is hereby stated that the Services are designed for and marketed to adults and are not intended to be accessed or used by children under 13, and that Vaultline does not intentionally or knowingly solicit, collect, store, or process personal information from children under 13. If you are a parent or guardian and believe that your child under 13 has provided us with personal information, please contact us at privacy@vaultline.com, and upon receiving such notification and verifying the claim to Vaultline\'s reasonable satisfaction, Vaultline will take commercially reasonable steps to identify and delete the personal information associated with the identified child\'s account, it being noted that such deletion may not be instantaneous and may require a reasonable period of time to effectuate given the technical complexity of identifying and removing all instances of such information from Vaultline\'s systems, backup archives, and the systems of third-party service providers with whom such information may have been shared prior to Vaultline\'s receipt of the parent or guardian\'s notification.

The foregoing notwithstanding, Vaultline may, in its sole discretion, elect to provide additional notice of material changes through such channels as it deems appropriate, but any such election shall not be construed as establishing a precedent or creating an obligation to provide similar notice with respect to any future changes.

**Contact Us.** If you have any questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us at: Vaultline Technologies, Inc., 1200 Lavaca Street, Suite 400, Austin, TX 78701. Email: privacy@vaultline.com. All inquiries and communications regarding privacy-related matters, including without limitation requests for information about the categories of personal information collected, questions about the data practices described in this Privacy Policy, and any other privacy-related questions or concerns, should be directed to the foregoing address or email address, and Vaultline will make commercially reasonable efforts to respond to such inquiries in a timely manner, typically within thirty business days of receipt, subject to the complexity of the inquiry, the volume of inquiries received, and any applicable legal requirements regarding response timelines, and it is noted that Vaultline may require that the identity of the inquiring party be verified prior to the provision of any substantive response, particularly with respect to requests that involve the disclosure of personal information or the exercise of privacy-related rights.

**Additional Legal Provisions.** This Privacy Policy shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Texas, without regard to its conflict of laws provisions, and any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Privacy Policy or the breach, termination, enforcement, interpretation, or validity thereof, including the determination of the scope or applicability of this agreement to arbitrate, shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in Travis County, Texas, and you hereby consent to the personal jurisdiction and venue of such courts and waive any objection based upon inconvenient forum or lack of jurisdiction, it being understood that the foregoing choice of law and forum selection provisions are intended to provide certainty and predictability with respect to the legal framework governing the interpretation and enforcement of this Privacy Policy and that such provisions shall be enforceable to the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, mutatis mutandis. If any provision of this Privacy Policy is held to be invalid or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect, and the invalid or unenforceable provision shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid and enforceable, or if such modification is not possible, shall be deemed severed from this Privacy Policy, and the validity and enforceability of the remaining provisions shall not be affected or impaired thereby. This Privacy Policy does not create any contractual or other legal right in or on behalf of any party, nor does it constitute a contract between Vaultline and any user, and nothing herein shall be construed as creating any third-party beneficiary rights or any agency, partnership, joint venture, or employment relationship between Vaultline and any user or any other person or entity. Our failure to enforce any provision of this Privacy Policy shall not be deemed a waiver of such provision or the right to enforce it, and no waiver of any provision hereof shall be effective unless it is made in writing and signed by a duly authorized representative of Vaultline, and any such waiver shall not be construed as a waiver of any subsequent breach or default of the same or any other provision of this Privacy Policy, ab initio. All provisions of this Privacy Policy that by their nature are intended to survive the termination or expiration of your relationship with Vaultline --- including without limitation provisions relating to data retention, limitation of liability, indemnification, choice of law, and dispute resolution --- shall survive and remain in full force and effect notwithstanding any such termination or expiration, and shall be enforceable by Vaultline and its successors and assigns.

**Supplemental Disclosures for Residents of Certain States.** Residents of Nevada may opt out of the sale of certain covered information as defined by Nevada Revised Statutes Chapter 603A by contacting us at privacy@vaultline.com, and Vaultline will process such requests in accordance with the requirements of applicable Nevada law, subject to verification of the requesting party\'s identity and Nevada residency, it being noted that Vaultline does not currently engage in the sale of covered information as defined by Nevada law but provides this disclosure and mechanism as a precautionary measure and in the interest of transparency. Residents of Virginia, Colorado, and Connecticut may have certain rights under the privacy laws of their respective states, including without limitation the Virginia Consumer Data Protection Act, the Colorado Privacy Act, and the Connecticut Data Privacy Act, and such rights may include, inter alia, rights related to the access, correction, deletion, and portability of personal data, as well as the right to opt out of certain processing activities, and the applicability and scope of such rights may depend upon a variety of factors, including without limitation whether Vaultline meets the jurisdictional thresholds established by such laws and the nature and context of the processing at issue. To exercise any rights you may have under the privacy laws of Virginia, Colorado, Connecticut, or any other applicable state, please contact us at privacy@vaultline.com, and Vaultline will review and respond to such requests in accordance with the requirements of applicable law, subject to the verification of the requesting party\'s identity and state residency and to any applicable exemptions, exceptions, or limitations set forth in the relevant statute and its implementing regulations.
